title: Why Mortgage Rates Are Going Up
summary: Mortgage rates in the UK are rising quickly, making it harder for people to borrow money for homes. This is the biggest change since 2022. The average rate for a two-year fixed mortgage is now above 5%, and many mortgage deals have been taken off the market. This affects people renewing their mortgages or buying their first home. The rise is linked to a war in the Middle East between the US, Israel, and Iran, which has caused oil prices to go up. Higher oil prices can lead to inflation, meaning things cost more. Lenders are increasing rates because they worry about future borrowing costs. Experts say this is bad news for borrowers and depends on how the conflict and markets change.

questions.question: How many mortgage products were pulled off the market recently?

background_read: Mortgages are loans people use to buy houses. When mortgage rates go up, it costs more to borrow money, making homes harder to afford. Inflation is when prices for things like food and gas increase, which can happen during conflicts like wars. The UK has seen big changes in mortgage rates before, such as in 2022. Understanding these ideas helps explain why news about rising rates matters for families and the economy.
Article_Structure: WHO: People in the UK renewing mortgages or buying first homes
WHAT: Mortgage rates are rising and deals are being pulled
WHY: Because of a war in the Middle East causing higher oil prices and inflation

perspectives.description: Borrowers are worried because rising rates make homes more expensive and loans harder to get.
